<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Mathematical statistics</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Estimators 2 MLE 3 Cramér–Rao 4 Testing 5 Bayesian 6 Asymptotics

Mathematical statistics

From samples to inference: estimators with bias and variance, maximum likelihood and the Fisher information bound, Neyman–Pearson tests, Bayesian posteriors with conjugate updates, and the asymptotic apparatus (delta method, Slutsky, Wilks) that makes finite-sample procedures honest as $n\to\infty$. Probability theory supplies the random variables; statistics asks what those samples tell you about the law.

blocks.html:
1. Point estimators: bias, consistency, MSE, sufficiency

Given i.i.d. samples $X_1,\ldots,X_n$ from a parametric family $\{f(x;\theta):\theta\in\Theta\}$, an estimator is any measurable function $\hat\theta_n=T(X_1,\ldots,X_n)$ taking values in $\Theta$. Two estimators of the same $\theta$ can differ wildly in their behaviour as $n$ grows — the language for comparing them is bias, variance, and risk.

The bias at $\theta$ is $b(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[\hat\theta_n]-\theta$; the variance is $\mathrm{Var}_\theta(\hat\theta_n)$; and the mean-square error decomposes: $$\mathrm{MSE}_\theta(\hat\theta_n)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[(\hat\theta_n-\theta)^2]=b(\theta)^2+\mathrm{Var}_\theta(\hat\theta_n).$$ A biased estimator with low variance can have lower MSE than an unbiased one — the statistical free lunch from accepting some bias.

Consistency. $\hat\theta_n$ is consistent if $\hat\theta_n\xrightarrow{p}\theta$ as $n\to\infty$. Strong consistency upgrades to almost-sure convergence. Consistency is essentially asking that bias and variance both go to zero.

Sufficiency. A statistic $T(X)$ is sufficient for $\theta$ when the conditional law of $X$ given $T$ is free of $\theta$. The Fisher–Neyman factorisation criterion makes this practical: $T$ is sufficient iff $$f(x;\theta)=g(T(x);\theta)\,h(x).$$ For i.i.d. $\mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2)$ samples, $(\sum X_i,\sum X_i^2)$ is sufficient — once you know the sample sum and sum of squares, the individual $X_i$ values carry no further information about $(\mu,\sigma^2)$.

Worked example: sample variance. Two natural variance estimators are $$\hat\sigma^2_{\mathrm{MLE}}=\tfrac{1}{n}\sum(X_i-\bar X)^2,\qquad S^2=\tfrac{1}{n-1}\sum(X_i-\bar X)^2.$$ The first has bias $-\sigma^2/n$ (downward); $S^2$ is unbiased — Bessel's correction. But $\hat\sigma^2_{\mathrm{MLE}}$ has smaller variance, so $\mathrm{MSE}(\hat\sigma^2_{\mathrm{MLE}})<\mathrm{MSE}(S^2)$ for moderate $n$. Bias is not the only thing to optimise.

Bias–variance tradeoff
two estimators of $\sigma^2$, $n=10$

See also
  • Expectation, variance, and moments
  • Random variables

<script>
/* =============================== §1 Bias-variance bars =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#mse-svg'), out = $('#mse-out');
  const sig = $('#mse-sigma'), nctl = $('#mse-n');
  const sigv = $('#mse-sigma-v'), nv = $('#mse-n-v');
  const W=760, H=280;
  function draw(){
    const sigma2 = +sig.value, n = +nctl.value;
    sigv.textContent = sigma2.toFixed(2);
    nv.textContent = n;
    // For Gaussian, Var(S^2) = 2 sigma^4/(n-1); Var(MLE) = (n-1)/n * Var(S^2) = 2(n-1)sigma^4/n^2
    // bias(MLE) = -sigma^2/n; bias(S^2) = 0
    const biasM = -sigma2/n, biasS = 0;
    const varM = 2*(n-1)*sigma2*sigma2/(n*n);
    const varS = 2*sigma2*sigma2/(n-1);
    const mseM = biasM*biasM + varM;
    const mseS = biasS*biasS + varS;
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const x0=80, y0=40, plotH=180, plotW=600;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0+plotH,x2:x0+plotW,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
    // labels
    const yt = SVG('text',{x:x0-50,y:y0+plotH/2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','transform':`rotate(-90 ${x0-50} ${y0+plotH/2})`});
    yt.textContent='MSE'; svg.appendChild(yt);
    const maxMSE = Math.max(mseM, mseS) * 1.15;
    function bar(x, w, biasSq, vari, label, color){
      const h1 = (biasSq/maxMSE)*plotH;
      const h2 = (vari/maxMSE)*plotH;
      // variance (bottom)
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x,y:y0+plotH-h2,width:w,height:h2,
        fill:color,opacity:0.85,stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':0.5}));
      // bias^2 (stacked on top)
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x,y:y0+plotH-h2-h1,width:w,height:h1,
        fill:'var(--pink)',opacity:0.85,stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':0.5}));
      const lt = SVG('text',{x:x+w/2,y:y0+plotH+22,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)'});
      lt.textContent = label; svg.appendChild(lt);
      const mt = SVG('text',{x:x+w/2,y:y0+plotH-h2-h1-8,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      mt.textContent = 'MSE='+(biasSq+vari).toFixed(3); svg.appendChild(mt);
    }
    bar(x0+120, 100, biasM*biasM, varM, 'MLE  (1/n)', 'var(--yellow)');
    bar(x0+340, 100, biasS*biasS, varS, 'S²  (1/(n-1))', 'var(--cyan)');
    // legend
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:x0+plotW-160,y:y0+10,width:14,height:14,fill:'var(--pink)',opacity:0.85}));
    const lt1 = SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-140,y:y0+22,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)'});
    lt1.textContent='bias²'; svg.appendChild(lt1);
    svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:x0+plotW-160,y:y0+32,width:14,height:14,fill:'var(--cyan)',opacity:0.85}));
    const lt2 = SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-140,y:y0+44,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--ink)'});
    lt2.textContent='variance'; svg.appendChild(lt2);
    out.textContent = `n=${n}, σ²=${sigma2.toFixed(2)}\nMLE: bias²=${(biasM*biasM).toFixed(4)}, var=${varM.toFixed(4)}, MSE=${mseM.toFixed(4)}\nS²:  bias²=${biasS.toFixed(4)},  var=${varS.toFixed(4)}, MSE=${mseS.toFixed(4)}\n${mseM<mseS?'MLE wins on MSE — accepted bias for less variance.':'S² wins on MSE.'}`;
  }
  [sig, nctl].forEach(el => el.addEventListener('input', draw));
  draw();
})();
</script>

2. Maximum likelihood and the score equation

The likelihood $L(\theta)=\prod_i f(X_i;\theta)$ is, viewed as a function of $\theta$ with the data fixed, just the joint density evaluated at the observed sample. Its log is the log-likelihood: $$\ell(\theta)=\sum_{i=1}^n\log f(X_i;\theta).$$ The maximum likelihood estimator is $\hat\theta_{\mathrm{MLE}}=\arg\max_\theta\ell(\theta)$. Differentiating and setting to zero gives the score equation: $$\partial_\theta\ell(\theta)=\sum_i\frac{\partial_\theta f(X_i;\theta)}{f(X_i;\theta)}=0.$$

Fisher information measures the curvature of the log-likelihood: $$I(\theta)=\mathbb{E}_\theta[(\partial_\theta\log f(X;\theta))^2]=-\mathbb{E}_\theta[\partial_\theta^2\log f(X;\theta)].$$ The two expressions agree under regularity (differentiating $\int f=1$ twice). The score has mean zero and variance $I(\theta)$.

Under regularity, the MLE is consistent and satisfies the asymptotic normality $$\sqrt n\bigl(\hat\theta_n-\theta\bigr)\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}\bigl(0,\,1/I(\theta)\bigr).$$ The Fisher information sets the asymptotic precision; we will see in §3 that it is also a hard floor.

MLE = empirical KL minimiser. Let $\hat p_n$ be the empirical distribution of the sample. Then $$D(\hat p_n\,\|\,p_\theta)=\mathbb{E}_{\hat p_n}[\log\hat p_n]-\tfrac{1}{n}\ell(\theta).$$ Minimising $D(\hat p_n\,\|\,p_\theta)$ in $\theta$ is the same problem as maximising $\ell(\theta)$. So MLE is the statistical shadow of KL divergence, and Sanov's theorem governs how fast the empirical law concentrates on the truth.

Worked example: exponential rate. For $X_i\sim\mathrm{Exp}(\lambda)$, $\log f=\log\lambda-\lambda x$, so $\ell(\lambda)=n\log\lambda-\lambda\sum X_i$ and $\hat\lambda_{\mathrm{MLE}}=n/\sum X_i=1/\bar X$. Fisher information is $I(\lambda)=1/\lambda^2$, so $\sqrt n(\hat\lambda-\lambda)\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}(0,\lambda^2)$.

Likelihood curve and score
Bernoulli($\theta$), drag samples

<script>
/* =============================== §2 Likelihood curve =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg = $('#mle-svg'), out = $('#mle-out');
  const kctl=$('#mle-k'), nctl=$('#mle-n');
  const kv=$('#mle-k-v'), nv=$('#mle-n-v');
  const W=760, H=320;
  function draw(){
    let n = +nctl.value, k = Math.min(+kctl.value, n);
    kv.textContent = k; nv.textContent = n;
    kctl.max = n;
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const x0=80, y0=40, plotW=600, plotH=220;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0+plotH,x2:x0+plotW,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    // x-axis ticks
    for(let i=0;i<=10;i++){
      const xx = x0 + i*plotW/10;
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xx,y1:y0+plotH,x2:xx,y2:y0+plotH+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xx,y:y0+plotH+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=(i/10).toFixed(1); svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const xlabel = SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW/2,y:y0+plotH+38,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xlabel.textContent='θ (heads probability)'; svg.appendChild(xlabel);
    const ylabel = SVG('text',{x:x0-50,y:y0+plotH/2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','transform':`rotate(-90 ${x0-50} ${y0+plotH/2})`});
    ylabel.textContent='log L(θ)'; svg.appendChild(ylabel);
    // log-likelihood curve
    function logL(theta){
      if(theta<=0 || theta>=1) return -1e9;
      return k*Math.log(theta) + (n-k)*Math.log(1-theta);
    }
    const N=200;
    const ys=[];
    for(let i=1;i<N;i++){
      const t = i/N;
      ys.push([t, logL(t)]);
    }
    const ymax = Math.max(...ys.map(p=>p[1]));
    const ymin = ymax - 8;
    function px(t){ return x0 + t*plotW; }
    function py(v){ return y0 + plotH - (v-ymin)/(ymax-ymin)*plotH; }
    let d='';
    ys.forEach((p,i)=>{
      const yp = Math.max(p[1], ymin);
      d += (i===0?'M':'L')+px(p[0])+' '+py(yp)+' ';
    });
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d, fill:'none', stroke:'var(--cyan)', 'stroke-width':2}));
    // MLE
    const mle = k/n;
    if(mle>0 && mle<1){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(mle),y1:y0,x2:px(mle),y2:y0+plotH,
        stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5,'stroke-dasharray':'4,4'}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px(mle),cy:py(logL(mle)),r:6,
        fill:'var(--yellow)',stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':1.5}));
      const lab=SVG('text',{x:px(mle)+8,y:y0+18,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
      lab.textContent='θ̂_MLE = '+mle.toFixed(3); svg.appendChild(lab);
    }
    // Asymptotic CI band: theta_hat ± 1.96/sqrt(n*I(theta))
    if(mle>0 && mle<1){
      const fisher = 1/(mle*(1-mle));
      const sd = 1/Math.sqrt(n*fisher);
      const lo = Math.max(0.001, mle - 1.96*sd);
      const hi = Math.min(0.999, mle + 1.96*sd);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('rect',{x:px(lo),y:y0+plotH-6,width:px(hi)-px(lo),height:6,
        fill:'var(--yellow)',opacity:0.35}));
      const ci=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-8,y:y0+plotH-12,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      ci.textContent=`95% CI ≈ [${lo.toFixed(3)}, ${hi.toFixed(3)}]`; svg.appendChild(ci);
    }
    out.textContent = `Bernoulli MLE: θ̂ = k/n = ${k}/${n} = ${mle.toFixed(4)}\nFisher info I(θ̂) = 1/(θ̂(1-θ̂)) = ${(mle>0&&mle<1?(1/(mle*(1-mle))).toFixed(3):'∞')}\nAsymptotic SD = 1/√(nI) = ${(mle>0&&mle<1?(1/Math.sqrt(n/(mle*(1-mle)))).toFixed(4):'—')}`;
  }
  [kctl, nctl].forEach(el => el.addEventListener('input', draw));
  draw();
})();
</script>

3. Cramér–Rao bound, UMVU, Rao–Blackwell

How small can $\mathrm{Var}_\theta(\hat\theta_n)$ be made among unbiased estimators? The Cramér–Rao inequality gives a sharp floor: $$\mathrm{Var}_\theta(\hat\theta_n)\ge\frac{1}{nI(\theta)}\quad\text{whenever}\quad\mathbb{E}_\theta[\hat\theta_n]=\theta.$$ Information bounds variance — there is no free lunch on precision below what the score equation allows.

An estimator achieving the floor is efficient; uniformly across $\theta$ it is the UMVU (uniformly minimum-variance unbiased) estimator. Sample mean $\bar X$ is UMVU for $\mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2)$'s mean — its variance $\sigma^2/n$ exactly equals $1/(nI)$.

Rao–Blackwell. If $W$ is unbiased and $T$ is sufficient, then $W^*=\mathbb{E}[W\mid T]$ is also unbiased and has $\mathrm{Var}(W^*)\le\mathrm{Var}(W)$. The proof is a one-line application of the conditional Jensen inequality plus the law of total variance: $$\mathrm{Var}(W)=\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{Var}(W\mid T)]+\mathrm{Var}(\mathbb{E}[W\mid T])\ge\mathrm{Var}(W^*).$$ Lehmann–Scheffé: if $T$ is also complete, $W^*$ is the unique UMVU estimator of $\theta$. Conditioning on a complete sufficient statistic projects you onto the optimal estimator.

Worked example. For $X_i\sim\mathcal{N}(\mu,\sigma^2)$ with $\sigma^2$ known, $I(\mu)=1/\sigma^2$ per sample, so the CRLB on unbiased estimators of $\mu$ is $\sigma^2/n$. The sample mean achieves this exactly — $\bar X$ is UMVU and efficient. For estimating $\sigma^2$ with $\mu$ known, the CRLB is $2\sigma^4/n$, and the MLE $\tfrac{1}{n}\sum(X_i-\mu)^2$ achieves it.

CRLB envelope
unbiased estimators live above the curve

<script>
/* =============================== §3 CRLB envelope =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#cr-svg'), out=$('#cr-out');
  const fctl=$('#cr-fisher'), fv=$('#cr-fisher-v');
  function draw(){
    const I = +fctl.value;
    fv.textContent = I.toFixed(2);
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const x0=80, y0=40, plotW=600, plotH=220;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0+plotH,x2:x0+plotW,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    const nMin=2, nMax=50;
    // y range: max at n=2
    const yMax = 1/(nMin*I) * 1.2;
    function px(n){ return x0 + (n-nMin)/(nMax-nMin)*plotW; }
    function py(v){ return y0 + plotH - Math.min(v,yMax)/yMax*plotH; }
    // CRLB curve
    let d='';
    for(let n=nMin;n<=nMax;n+=0.5){
      const v = 1/(n*I);
      d += (n===nMin?'M':'L')+px(n)+' '+py(v)+' ';
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':2.5}));
    // Fill below CRLB (forbidden)
    let dfill = d + ' L'+px(nMax)+' '+py(yMax)+' L'+px(nMin)+' '+py(yMax)+' Z';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dfill,fill:'var(--pink)',opacity:0.08}));
    // Sample "estimator" points: efficient (on curve), inefficient (above)
    function dot(n, v, color, label){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px(n),cy:py(v),r:5,fill:color,stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':1}));
      if(label){
        const t=SVG('text',{x:px(n)+8,y:py(v)+4,'font-size':11,fill:color});
        t.textContent=label; svg.appendChild(t);
      }
    }
    [5,10,20,40].forEach(n => dot(n, 1/(n*I), 'var(--yellow)', null));
    dot(10, 1.5/(10*I), 'var(--pink)', 'inefficient');
    dot(20, 1.0/(20*I), 'var(--cyan)', 'efficient');
    // labels
    for(let n=10;n<=50;n+=10){
      const t=SVG('text',{x:px(n),y:y0+plotH+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=n; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const xlabel=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW/2,y:y0+plotH+38,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xlabel.textContent='sample size n'; svg.appendChild(xlabel);
    const ylabel=SVG('text',{x:x0-50,y:y0+plotH/2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','transform':`rotate(-90 ${x0-50} ${y0+plotH/2})`});
    ylabel.textContent='Var(θ̂)'; svg.appendChild(ylabel);
    // Legend (viewport coords)
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-160,y:y0+18,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'}));
    const lg1=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-150,y:y0+22,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    lg1.textContent='1/(nI(θ))  CRLB'; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-150,y:y0+40,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'});
    lg2.textContent='forbidden region'; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    out.textContent = `Fisher info I(θ) = ${I.toFixed(2)}\nCRLB at n=10:  Var(θ̂) ≥ ${(1/(10*I)).toFixed(4)}\nCRLB at n=20:  Var(θ̂) ≥ ${(1/(20*I)).toFixed(4)}\nUMVU/efficient estimators sit ON the yellow curve;\nany unbiased estimator must lie above it.`;
  }
  fctl.addEventListener('input', draw);
  draw();
})();
</script>

4. Hypothesis testing: Neyman–Pearson and likelihood ratios

A statistical test partitions the sample space into a rejection region $R$ and its complement; we reject $H_0:\theta\in\Theta_0$ in favour of $H_1:\theta\in\Theta_1$ when $X\in R$. Two error types:

$H_0$ true$H_1$ true
reject $H_0$type I, prob $\alpha$correct, prob $1-\beta$ (power)
accept $H_0$correct, prob $1-\alpha$type II, prob $\beta$

The size $\alpha=\sup_{\theta\in\Theta_0}\mathbb{P}_\theta(X\in R)$ is the worst-case type-I error rate; the power function is $\theta\mapsto\mathbb{P}_\theta(X\in R)$, which we want large on $\Theta_1$ for fixed $\alpha$.

Neyman–Pearson lemma. For simple-vs-simple $H_0:\theta=\theta_0$ vs $H_1:\theta=\theta_1$, the level-$\alpha$ test that rejects when $$\Lambda(x)=\frac{L(\theta_1;x)}{L(\theta_0;x)}>k$$ (with $k$ chosen so $\mathbb{P}_{\theta_0}(\Lambda>k)=\alpha$) is uniformly most powerful: every other test of size $\le\alpha$ has power $\le 1-\beta_{\mathrm{NP}}$. Likelihood ratios are the optimal test statistic.

The $p$-value of an observation $x_{\mathrm{obs}}$ is $p=\mathbb{P}_{H_0}(T(X)\ge T(x_{\mathrm{obs}}))$ for the chosen test statistic $T$ — the smallest level at which $x_{\mathrm{obs}}$ would have led to rejection. It is not $\mathbb{P}(H_0\mid x)$.

Worked example: one-sample z-test. Test $H_0:\mu=0$ vs $H_1:\mu>0$ with i.i.d.\ $X_i\sim\mathcal{N}(\mu,1)$ and $n=25$. Under $H_0$, $\bar X\sim\mathcal{N}(0,1/25)$; reject at level $0.05$ when $\bar X>z_{0.95}/\sqrt n=1.645/5\approx 0.329$. Power at $\mu=0.5$: $$\mathbb{P}_{0.5}(\bar X>0.329)=1-\Phi\bigl(\tfrac{0.329-0.5}{1/5}\bigr)=1-\Phi(-0.855)\approx 0.804.$$

Neyman–Pearson rejection region
slide $\alpha$, watch power follow

<script>
/* =============================== §4 Neyman-Pearson regions =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#np-svg'), out=$('#np-out');
  const mu1=$('#np-mu1'), alpha=$('#np-alpha');
  const mv=$('#np-mu1-v'), av=$('#np-alpha-v');
  function invPhi(p){
    // Beasley-Springer-Moro inversion (rough but adequate)
    if(p<=0) return -10; if(p>=1) return 10;
    const a = [-3.969683028665376e+01,2.209460984245205e+02,-2.759285104469687e+02,
               1.383577518672690e+02,-3.066479806614716e+01,2.506628277459239e+00];
    const b = [-5.447609879822406e+01,1.615858368580409e+02,-1.556989798598866e+02,
               6.680131188771972e+01,-1.328068155288572e+01];
    const c = [-7.784894002430293e-03,-3.223964580411365e-01,-2.400758277161838e+00,
               -2.549732539343734e+00,4.374664141464968e+00,2.938163982698783e+00];
    const d = [7.784695709041462e-03,3.224671290700398e-01,2.445134137142996e+00,
               3.754408661907416e+00];
    const pl=0.02425, ph=1-pl;
    let q,r;
    if(p<pl){
      q=Math.sqrt(-2*Math.log(p));
      return (((((c[0]*q+c[1])*q+c[2])*q+c[3])*q+c[4])*q+c[5])
            /((((d[0]*q+d[1])*q+d[2])*q+d[3])*q+1);
    }
    if(p<=ph){
      q=p-0.5; r=q*q;
      return (((((a[0]*r+a[1])*r+a[2])*r+a[3])*r+a[4])*r+a[5])*q
            /(((((b[0]*r+b[1])*r+b[2])*r+b[3])*r+b[4])*r+1);
    }
    q=Math.sqrt(-2*Math.log(1-p));
    return -(((((c[0]*q+c[1])*q+c[2])*q+c[3])*q+c[4])*q+c[5])
            /((((d[0]*q+d[1])*q+d[2])*q+d[3])*q+1);
  }
  function draw(){
    const m1=+mu1.value, a=+alpha.value;
    mv.textContent=m1.toFixed(2); av.textContent=a.toFixed(3);
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const x0=80, y0=40, plotW=600, plotH=220;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0+plotH,x2:x0+plotW,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    const xMin=-3, xMax=4;
    function px(x){ return x0 + (x-xMin)/(xMax-xMin)*plotW; }
    const peakY = phi(0);
    function py(v){ return y0 + plotH - v/peakY*(plotH*0.92); }
    // critical value: under H0~N(0,1), reject when X > z_{1-a}
    const z = invPhi(1-a);
    // H0 curve
    let d0='';
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){
      const x=xMin+i/200*(xMax-xMin);
      d0 += (i===0?'M':'L')+px(x)+' '+py(phi(x))+' ';
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:d0,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--cyan)','stroke-width':2}));
    // H1 curve (shifted by mu1)
    let d1='';
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){
      const x=xMin+i/200*(xMax-xMin);
      d1 += (i===0?'M':'L')+px(x)+' '+py(phi(x-m1))+' ';
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:d1,fill:'none',stroke:'var(--pink)','stroke-width':2}));
    // Type I (alpha): area under H0 to the right of z
    let dA='M'+px(z)+' '+py(0);
    for(let i=0;i<=80;i++){
      const x=z+i/80*(xMax-z);
      dA += ' L'+px(x)+' '+py(phi(x));
    }
    dA += ' L'+px(xMax)+' '+py(0)+' Z';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dA,fill:'var(--cyan)',opacity:0.35}));
    // Type II (beta): area under H1 to the LEFT of z
    let dB='M'+px(xMin)+' '+py(0);
    for(let i=0;i<=80;i++){
      const x=xMin+i/80*(z-xMin);
      dB += ' L'+px(x)+' '+py(phi(x-m1));
    }
    dB += ' L'+px(z)+' '+py(0)+' Z';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dB,fill:'var(--pink)',opacity:0.25}));
    // critical line
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(z),y1:y0,x2:px(z),y2:y0+plotH,
      stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5,'stroke-dasharray':'4,4'}));
    const cz=SVG('text',{x:px(z),y:y0-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    cz.textContent='z='+z.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(cz);
    // x ticks
    for(let x=xMin;x<=xMax;x++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(x),y1:y0+plotH,x2:px(x),y2:y0+plotH+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:px(x),y:y0+plotH+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=x; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // legend (viewport coords)
    const lg1=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-130,y:y0+18,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lg1.textContent='H₀: N(0,1)'; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-130,y:y0+34,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'});
    lg2.textContent=`H₁: N(${m1.toFixed(1)},1)`; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    // power
    const beta = Phi(z-m1);
    const power = 1-beta;
    out.textContent = `α = ${a.toFixed(3)}  (cyan area, type I)\nβ = ${beta.toFixed(3)}  (pink area, type II)\npower = 1−β = ${power.toFixed(3)}\nrejection region: X > ${z.toFixed(3)}`;
  }
  [mu1, alpha].forEach(el => el.addEventListener('input', draw));
  draw();
})();
</script>

5. Bayesian inference: posteriors, conjugate priors, credible intervals

Bayesian inference treats $\theta$ as a random variable. Begin with a prior $\pi(\theta)$ encoding belief before data; observe $X$ with likelihood $L(\theta;x)=f(x;\theta)$; the posterior is $$\pi(\theta\mid x)=\frac{\pi(\theta)L(\theta;x)}{\int\pi(\theta')L(\theta';x)\,d\theta'}\propto\pi(\theta)L(\theta;x).$$ Bayes' rule on parameters — the same identity that runs the medical-test posterior in probability theory, applied to a continuous parameter.

Conjugate priors are families closed under updating: when prior and likelihood multiply to a posterior in the same family, only the hyperparameters change. The classics:

likelihoodconjugate priorposterior
Binomial$(n,\theta)$$\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha,\beta)$$\mathrm{Beta}(\alpha+x,\beta+n-x)$
Poisson$(\lambda)$$\mathrm{Gamma}(\alpha,\beta)$$\mathrm{Gamma}(\alpha+\sum x_i,\beta+n)$
Normal$(\mu,\sigma^2)$, $\sigma^2$ knownNormal$(\mu_0,\tau_0^2)$Normal (precisions add)

Point summaries of the posterior:

  • MAP $\hat\theta_{\mathrm{MAP}}=\arg\max\pi(\theta\mid x)$ — posterior mode; minimises a $0$–$1$ loss.
  • Posterior mean $\mathbb{E}[\theta\mid x]$ — minimises squared-error loss.
  • Posterior median — minimises absolute-error loss.

For symmetric unimodal posteriors all three coincide. A $1-\alpha$ credible interval $C$ is any region with $\mathbb{P}(\theta\in C\mid x)=1-\alpha$; the highest posterior density interval is the shortest such region.

Frequentist vs Bayesian, in one line. A frequentist confidence interval has a coverage guarantee over resampling data with $\theta$ fixed; a Bayesian credible interval is a probability statement about $\theta$ given the observed data. They agree numerically with flat priors and Gaussian likelihoods; they diverge philosophically and at small $n$.

Worked example. Coin with $\theta\sim\mathrm{Beta}(1,1)$ (uniform prior). Flip $n=10$ times, see $k=7$ heads. Posterior is $\mathrm{Beta}(8,4)$, with mean $8/12\approx 0.667$ and mode $7/10=0.700$ (= MLE). The MLE is the MAP under the flat prior — uniform priors recover frequentist point estimates as their posterior modes.

Beta posterior update
prior + new flips → posterior

<script>
/* =============================== §5 Bayesian update =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#bayes-svg'), out=$('#ba-out');
  const pa=$('#ba-prior-a'), pb=$('#ba-prior-b');
  const kctl=$('#ba-k'), nctl=$('#ba-n');
  const pav=$('#ba-prior-a-v'), pbv=$('#ba-prior-b-v');
  const kv=$('#ba-k-v'), nv=$('#ba-n-v');
  function draw(){
    const a=+pa.value, b=+pb.value;
    let n=+nctl.value, k=Math.min(+kctl.value, n);
    pav.textContent=a.toFixed(1); pbv.textContent=b.toFixed(1);
    kv.textContent=k; nv.textContent=n;
    kctl.max=n;
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const x0=80, y0=40, plotW=600, plotH=220;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0+plotH,x2:x0+plotW,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    function curve(alpha,beta,N){
      const out=[];
      for(let i=1;i<N;i++){
        const t=i/N;
        out.push([t, betaPdf(t,alpha,beta)]);
      }
      return out;
    }
    const N=240;
    const post_a = a+k, post_b = b+n-k;
    const c0 = curve(a,b,N);
    const cP = curve(post_a, post_b, N);
    // likelihood (normalised) ~ Beta(k+1, n-k+1)
    const cL = curve(k+1, n-k+1, N);
    const yMax = Math.max(...c0.map(p=>p[1]), ...cP.map(p=>p[1]), ...cL.map(p=>p[1])) * 1.1;
    function px(t){ return x0 + t*plotW; }
    function py(v){ return y0 + plotH - Math.min(v,yMax)/yMax*plotH; }
    function plot(arr, color, dash, fillOp){
      let d='';
      arr.forEach((p,i)=>{ d += (i===0?'M':'L')+px(p[0])+' '+py(p[1])+' '; });
      const path = SVG('path',{d, fill: fillOp ? color : 'none', stroke:color,'stroke-width':2, opacity: fillOp || 1});
      if(dash) path.setAttribute('stroke-dasharray', dash);
      svg.appendChild(path);
    }
    plot(c0, 'var(--violet)', '5,4');         // prior
    plot(cL, 'var(--cyan)',   '2,3');         // likelihood
    plot(cP, 'var(--yellow)', null);          // posterior
    // posterior fill (credible region 95% via numeric quantile approximation)
    // simple approach: fill 95% central
    // x ticks
    for(let i=0;i<=10;i++){
      const xx=px(i/10);
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:xx,y1:y0+plotH,x2:xx,y2:y0+plotH+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:xx,y:y0+plotH+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});

    }
    const xlabel=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW/2,y:y0+plotH+38,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xlabel.textContent='θ'; svg.appendChild(xlabel);
    // posterior mean and mode markers
    const postMean = post_a/(post_a+post_b);
    const postMode = (post_a>1 && post_b>1) ? (post_a-1)/(post_a+post_b-2) : null;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(postMean),y1:y0+plotH,x2:px(postMean),y2:py(betaPdf(postMean,post_a,post_b)),
      stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1}));
    if(postMode!==null){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('circle',{cx:px(postMode),cy:py(betaPdf(postMode,post_a,post_b)),r:5,
        fill:'var(--yellow)',stroke:'var(--ink)','stroke-width':1}));
    }
    // legend (viewport coords)
    const lg1=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-150,y:y0+18,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--violet)'});
    lg1.textContent=`prior  Beta(${a.toFixed(1)}, ${b.toFixed(1)})`; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-150,y:y0+34,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lg2.textContent=`likelihood (${k}/${n})`; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    const lg3=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-150,y:y0+50,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    lg3.textContent=`posterior  Beta(${post_a.toFixed(1)}, ${post_b.toFixed(1)})`; svg.appendChild(lg3);
    out.textContent = `Posterior: Beta(${post_a.toFixed(1)}, ${post_b.toFixed(1)})\nposterior mean = ${postMean.toFixed(4)}\nposterior mode (MAP) = ${postMode!==null?postMode.toFixed(4):'(at boundary)'}\nMLE k/n = ${(k/n).toFixed(4)}`;
  }

</script>

6. Asymptotics: delta method, Slutsky, Wilks

The CLT promotes any sample statistic with finite variance to an asymptotic Gaussian. Mathematical statistics builds three workhorses on top of it.

Delta method. If $\sqrt n(\hat\theta_n-\theta)\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}(0,\sigma^2)$ and $g$ is continuously differentiable at $\theta$ with $g'(\theta)\neq 0$, then $$\sqrt n\bigl(g(\hat\theta_n)-g(\theta)\bigr)\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}\bigl(0,\,g'(\theta)^2\sigma^2\bigr).$$ Proof: Taylor expand $g(\hat\theta_n)\approx g(\theta)+g'(\theta)(\hat\theta_n-\theta)$ and apply Slutsky. The Jacobian rescales the asymptotic variance.

Slutsky's theorem. If $X_n\xrightarrow{d}X$ and $Y_n\xrightarrow{p}c$ (constant), then $X_n+Y_n\xrightarrow{d}X+c$ and $X_nY_n\xrightarrow{d}cX$. The lemma that lets you replace consistent nuisance estimates by their probability limits inside a converging sequence — for example, $\sqrt n(\bar X-\mu)/S\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}(0,1)$ because $S\xrightarrow{p}\sigma$.

Wilks' theorem. For the LRT statistic $\Lambda_n=\sup_{\Theta_0}L/\sup_\Theta L$ with $H_0$ specifying $r=\dim\Theta-\dim\Theta_0$ constraints, under $H_0$: $$-2\log\Lambda_n\xrightarrow{d}\chi^2_r.$$ This is the asymptotic calibration of the LRT — without simulation, you can read off the rejection threshold from $\chi^2_r$ tables. The constant $-2$ comes from Taylor-expanding $\log L$ around the unrestricted MLE; Fisher information turns the quadratic into a sum of $r$ squared standard normals.

Worked example. Estimate $\theta=p^2$ for $p$ a binomial proportion. The MLE of $p$ is $\hat p=k/n$ with $\sqrt n(\hat p-p)\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}(0,p(1-p))$. Apply the delta method with $g(p)=p^2$, $g'(p)=2p$: $$\sqrt n(\hat p^2-p^2)\xrightarrow{d}\mathcal{N}(0,4p^2\cdot p(1-p))=\mathcal{N}(0,4p^3(1-p)).$$ At $p=1/2$, this is $\mathcal{N}(0,1/4)$.

Wilks' theorem visualisation
$-2\log\Lambda$ pools to $\chi^2_r$

See also
  • Central limit theorem
  • Modes of convergence

<script>
/* =============================== §6 Wilks chi-squared =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#asy-svg'), out=$('#asy-out');
  const rctl=$('#asy-r'), actl=$('#asy-alpha');
  const rv=$('#asy-r-v'), av=$('#asy-alpha-v');
  function chi2pdf(x, r){
    if(x<=0) return 0;
    return Math.exp((r/2-1)*Math.log(x) - x/2 - logGamma(r/2) - (r/2)*Math.log(2));
  }
  // crude inverse via bisection on lower-tail integral
  function chi2cdf(x, r){
    // numerical integral via Simpson on [0,x]
    if(x<=0) return 0;
    const N=200;
    const h=x/N;
    let s=chi2pdf(0,r)+chi2pdf(x,r);
    for(let i=1;i<N;i++){
      s += (i%2===0?2:4)*chi2pdf(i*h, r);
    }
    return s*h/3;
  }
  function chi2inv(p, r){
    // bisection
    let lo=0, hi=Math.max(20, 2*r+20);
    for(let i=0;i<60;i++){
      const m=(lo+hi)/2;
      if(chi2cdf(m, r) < p) lo=m; else hi=m;
    }
    return (lo+hi)/2;
  }
  function draw(){
    const r=+rctl.value, a=+actl.value;
    rv.textContent=r; av.textContent=a.toFixed(3);
    svg.innerHTML='';
    const x0=80, y0=40, plotW=600, plotH=220;
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0+plotH,x2:x0+plotW,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0,y1:y0,x2:x0,y2:y0+plotH,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
    const xMax = Math.max(15, 3*r+8);
    function px(x){ return x0 + x/xMax*plotW; }
    // y range
    let yMax = 0;
    const N=300;
    for(let i=1;i<=N;i++){
      const x = i/N*xMax;
      const v = chi2pdf(x, r);
      if(v>yMax) yMax=v;
    }
    yMax *= 1.15;
    function py(v){ return y0 + plotH - Math.min(v,yMax)/yMax*plotH; }
    // pdf curve
    let d='';
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      const x = i/N*xMax;
      d += (i===0?'M':'L')+px(x)+' '+py(chi2pdf(x, r))+' ';
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d, fill:'none', stroke:'var(--cyan)', 'stroke-width':2}));
    // critical value
    const crit = chi2inv(1-a, r);
    // shade tail
    let dT='M'+px(crit)+' '+py(0);
    for(let i=0;i<=80;i++){
      const x = crit + i/80*(xMax-crit);
      dT += ' L'+px(x)+' '+py(chi2pdf(x, r));
    }
    dT += ' L'+px(xMax)+' '+py(0)+' Z';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('path',{d:dT, fill:'var(--pink)', opacity:0.35}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(crit),y1:y0,x2:px(crit),y2:y0+plotH,
      stroke:'var(--yellow)','stroke-width':1.5,'stroke-dasharray':'4,4'}));
    const cl=SVG('text',{x:px(crit),y:y0-6,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--yellow)'});
    cl.textContent='χ²_{r,1−α} = '+crit.toFixed(2); svg.appendChild(cl);
    // x ticks
    const step = Math.max(1, Math.floor(xMax/8));
    for(let x=0;x<=xMax;x+=step){
      svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:px(x),y1:y0+plotH,x2:px(x),y2:y0+plotH+4,stroke:'var(--mute)'}));
      const t=SVG('text',{x:px(x),y:y0+plotH+18,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent=x; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const xlabel=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW/2,y:y0+plotH+38,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)'});
    xlabel.textContent='−2 log Λₙ  (LRT statistic under H₀)'; svg.appendChild(xlabel);
    const ylabel=SVG('text',{x:x0-50,y:y0+plotH/2,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','transform':`rotate(-90 ${x0-50} ${y0+plotH/2})`});
    ylabel.textContent='χ²_r density'; svg.appendChild(ylabel);
    // legend
    const lg1=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-160,y:y0+18,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--cyan)'});
    lg1.textContent=`χ²_${r} pdf`; svg.appendChild(lg1);
    const lg2=SVG('text',{x:x0+plotW-160,y:y0+34,'font-size':11,fill:'var(--pink)'});
    lg2.textContent=`reject region (α=${a.toFixed(3)})`; svg.appendChild(lg2);
    out.textContent = `r = ${r}  (= dim Θ − dim Θ₀, # constrained parameters)\nα = ${a.toFixed(3)}\ncritical value χ²_{r,1−α} = ${crit.toFixed(3)}\nReject H₀ when −2 log Λₙ > ${crit.toFixed(3)}.`;
  }
  [rctl, actl].forEach(el => el.addEventListener('input', draw));
  draw();
})();
</script>

Connections

Mathematical statistics is the inferential face of probability — every result here treats samples drawn from a probability law and asks what those samples reveal. Live frontiers: high-dimensional asymptotics where $p/n$ does not vanish (random matrix corrections to MLE), causal inference (where conditional distributions encode counterfactuals, not just associations), and the bridge to learning theory (PAC bounds, empirical risk minimisation as MLE under model misspecification).

  • Probability theory — central limit theorem — the asymptotic engine that makes MLE, delta method, and Wilks calibrate.
  • Information theory — KL divergence — MLE is empirical-KL minimisation, and Sanov's theorem governs how fast empirical laws concentrate.
  • Large deviations — Sanov's theorem — sharp exponential tail bounds for likelihood ratios; refines Wilks beyond first order.
  • Stochastic processes — filtrations — sequential testing and martingale-based confidence sequences extend the static framework here.
  • Information theory — mutual information — Fisher information is the local quadratic form of KL, hence the second derivative of relative entropy.
